reviewer: retrospective critique backfill (code-grounded + existing conformance suite), revision 1

rubric.evidence: worker.js loads a REAL transformers.js text-classification pipeline (loadPipeline({task:'text-classification', model:'onnx-community/xlm-roberta-base-language-detection-ONNX', backend:'wasm', dtype:'q8'})) and calls pipe(text, {top_k: 20}) on-device — the papluca/xlm-roberta-base-language-detection model exported to ONNX. Conformance calls-real-inference (source) + loads-clean (console-clean) tested pass; io-shape-semantic manual-evidenced. No measured latency fabricated.
rubric.notes: Because XLM-RoBERTa is multilingual (100 languages share ONE embedding space), it reads real linguistic signal — script, morphology, function words — not a per-language keyword list, so it labels short snippets and stays honest (spreads probability) on ambiguous/code-switched input.

rubric.evidence: Expected output = a classification distribution over 20 languages. worker.js calls pipe(text, {top_k: 20}) so it returns the FULL softmax distribution (sorted high→low) rather than only the top label — the page shows every language's probability. io-shape-semantic conformance assertion covers a real language distribution.
rubric.notes: Returning the full 20-way distribution (not just top-1) is what lets the See-inside surface show honest uncertainty on ambiguous input.

rubric.evidence: At-a-glance declares onnx-community/xlm-roberta-base-language-detection-ONNX (linked, 5 occurrences), the text-classification task, q8 dtype, WASM backend, ~270 MB and MIT license — matching models.json. declares-model-id/task/backend/quantisation conformance assertions tested pass.
rubric.notes: This is the ONNX export of papluca/xlm-roberta-base-language-detection, disclosed in the worker header. 20-language fine-tune. Inference off the main thread.

rubric.evidence: The page uses the shared createModelLoader auto-init (modelId onnx-community/xlm-roberta-base-language-detection-ONNX, q8, ~270 MB, requiresWebGPU:false); a valid current cached copy auto-initialises with no Load click. shared-auto-init-loader conformance assertion tested pass.
rubric.notes: ~270 MB — the never-silently-re-download guarantee + Clear-cache control matter; size surfaced in at-a-glance.

rubric.evidence: runStatus role=status aria-live; worker errors surface on the page; shared loader adds checking→initialising→ready + Retry. live-status-region + errors-on-the-page conformance assertions tested pass; honest-progress-retry-offline / runs-without-webgpu-fallback manual-evidenced.
Real, labelled controls: the text <textarea>, sample-text chips, and a Run button. All keyboard-operable. controls-operable conformance assertion is manual-evidenced.
rubric.notes: Sample chips in multiple scripts are a good way to demonstrate the model reads script/morphology rather than keywords.

rubric.evidence: No canned scores: the full 20-way probability distribution shown as bars is the real pipeline softmax output. no-fake-output conformance assertion is manual-evidenced.
rubric.notes: Showing the whole distribution (not a hand-picked top-1) is itself an anti-fake, honest-uncertainty design.

rubric.evidence: Run button + controls carry names (buttons-have-names tested pass); status role=status aria-live; result region aria-live; page lang=en; WCAG-AA light+dark. images-have-alt tested pass.
rubric.notes: Probability bars are DOM with text percentages, inspectable by assistive tech.

rubric.evidence: responsive-desktop/responsive-mobile (no-horizontal-overflow) conformance assertions present; design-system panels + a 20-row distribution that reflows. models.json support is needs-review on both classes (automated pre-scan flagged sub-24px tap targets), pending an agent matrix pass to confirm chip/button tap sizes and flip to ok.
rubric.notes: The 20-row distribution is tall on mobile; recorded honestly as needs-review.

rubric.evidence: Classification runs in the Web Worker; the main thread only renders the 20 probability bars. inference-off-main-thread conformance assertion tested pass; inp-stays-low manual-evidenced.
rubric.notes: A single forward pass per input — light compute; off-thread so INP stays low.

guidanceConsulted.query: keep INP low move heavy work off main thread web worker
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Run inference off the main thread; keep INP low.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the classification forward pass runs in the worker; the main thread only paints the 20-way distribution.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: worker.js off-thread; inference-off-main-thread conformance assertion tested pass

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible form labels text input textarea
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Label the input; keep controls keyboard-operable; provide quick sample inputs.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the text textarea is labelled, sample chips + Run are keyboard-operable.

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible live region status role status aria-live
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce state via role=status aria-live; give controls accessible names.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — runStatus role=status aria-live; result region aria-live; Run button named.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: live-status-region + buttons-have-names conformance assertions tested pass

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive control panel without horizontal overflow flex grid
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Fluid layout with wrapping; keep the tall distribution list within its container.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — design-system panels; the 20-row distribution reflows and never forces horizontal overflow.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: responsive assertions present; support needs-review pending agent matrix pass

openQuestions: The fine-tune covers 20 languages — should the UI make the closed-set nature explicit (input outside the 20 is forced into the nearest of the 20), so users don't over-read a confident label on an unsupported language?
Is the tall 20-row distribution comfortable on a 360px viewport, or should low-probability rows collapse behind a 'show all' on mobile?

followUpGoals.goal: State the closed 20-language set explicitly in the UI and consider collapsing near-zero-probability rows on mobile to shorten the distribution list.
Run the mobile+desktop responsive matrix, confirm/raise chip and button tap targets, and flip models.json support to ok.

summary: Retrospective, code-grounded revision-1 critique for language-identification (onnx-community/xlm-roberta-base-language-detection-ONNX, text-classification, q8, ~270 MB, MIT — the ONNX export of papluca/xlm-roberta-base-language-detection, 20 languages). Real classification: worker.js runs the transformers.js text-classification pipeline off the main thread with top_k=20 so it returns the FULL softmax distribution (honest uncertainty on ambiguous/code-switched input), rendered as 20 probability bars. Auto-init via createModelLoader; role=status aria-live states; sample chips in multiple scripts; no fake output. Verified against the immutable conformance suite (calls-real-inference, loads-clean, declares-*, inference-off-main-thread, shared-auto-init-loader, live-status-region, buttons-have-names tested pass; io-shape/parity/inp manual-evidenced). No fabricated numbers. Honest weaknesses: closed 20-language set (out-of-set input is forced into the nearest label — worth stating explicitly in the UI); the tall 20-row distribution is long on narrow mobile; models.json support needs-review pending a tap-target matrix pass.
